entries.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Use appropriate grammar and vocabulary in oral and in written language tasks
entries.learning_activity: (a) Prepare and deliver short speeches about common topics (e.g., entrepreneurship, corruption, child rights and security, gender education, cyber security education, disaster management, health and reproductive education, drug abuse, life skills, financial management education, taxation)
teaching_learning_activities: Project work: Ask the students to listen to various short speeches in their environment or from online sources and analyse them based on their characteristics, parts, the language used and techniques used in delivering such speeches.
Presentation: Guide the students through presenting their work to the class
Observation: Guide the students through observing and discussing the characteristics and language used in a given short speech on common topics
Speech delivery: Guide the students through preparing short speeches on common topics and delivering such speeches to the class using appropriate techniques
entries.assessment: Short speeches about common topics are prepared and delivered appropriately
resources: audio and audio-visual materials (audio/video clips, recorded stories)
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Use appropriate grammar and vocabulary in oral and in written language tasks
entries.learning_activity: (b) Compose different types of essays (narrative, descriptive, argumentative and expository) using correct grammar and vocabulary
teaching_learning_activities: Observation: Guide the students through observing and discussing the characteristics and language used in different types of essays. Then, let each group present its work to the class
Composing/essay writing : Guide the students through composing different types of essays based on their characteristics
entries.assessment: Different types of essays are composed using correct grammar and vocabulary
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Use appropriate grammar and vocabulary in oral and in written language tasks
entries.learning_activity: (b) Compose different types of essays (narrative, descriptive, argumentative and expository) using correct grammar and vocabulary
teaching_learning_activities: Observation: Guide the students through observing and discussing the characteristics and language used in different types of essays. Then, let each group present its work to the class
Composing/essay writing : Guide the students through composing different types of essays based on their characteristics
entries.assessment: Different types of essays are composed using correct grammar and vocabulary
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 2.1 Read texts for comprehension
entries.learning_activity: (b) Deliver an oral presentation about the content of a text
teaching_learning_activities: Discussion: Guide the students through identifying and discussing the details of a given text and presenting their work to the class
entries.assessment: An oral presentation about the content of the text is made appropriately
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 2.2 Comprehend oral messages with increasing difficulty
entries.learning_activity: (a) Deduce the main ideas from an oral presentation or language task
teaching_learning_activities: Modelling: Play a recorded text/ speech to the students and show them how to deduce the main ideas from the text/speech

Reading aloud: Ask another teacher to read aloud a text or deliver a speech while you listen to it attentively with your students. Then, show the students how to deduce the main ideas from such text/speech

Speech delivery/Presentation: Deliver a speech/make a presentation to the students on common or cross-cutting topics using appropriate techniques and guide them through deducing the main ideas from the speech/presentation and presenting their work to the class
entries.assessment: The main ideas from oral presentations and language tasks are deduced appropriately
resources: audio and audio-visual materials (audio/video clips, recorded stories)
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 2.2 Comprehend oral messages with increasing difficulty
entries.learning_activity: (b) Examine information from oral sources
teaching_learning_activities: Pair work: Read aloud a story/ passage to the students and guide them in pairs through examining information from the story/ passage and presenting their work to the class
Group work: Play a recorded text/speech to the students and guide them in groups through examining the information obtained in the text/speech and presenting their work to the class

Project activity: Ask the students to listen to and watch the news on the radio and TV respectively at home or school. Then, ask them to examine the information from the news broadcast and present their work to the class
entries.assessment: Information from oral sources is examined appropriately
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 2.3 Construct meaning from a variety of texts
entries.learning_activity: (a) Explore strategies for deriving meanings of words from given texts (e.g., contextual guessing)
teaching_learning_activities: Exploration: Guide the students through exploring strategies for deriving meanings of words from texts and presenting their work to the class
entries.assessment: Strategies for deriving meanings of words from given texts are explored appropriately
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 2.3 Construct meaning from a variety of texts
entries.learning_activity: (d) Make inferences about a variety of texts
teaching_learning_activities: Group work : Guide the students in groups through finding the details of the texts, using such details and personal knowledge to make inferences and presenting their work to the class
entries.assessment: Inferences about a variety of texts are made appropriately
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 3.1 Use appropriate grammar and vocabulary for oral communication in a variety of contexts
entries.learning_activity: (a) Express feelings and emotions depicted/portrayed in various social contexts using appropriate phrases (e.g., I am saddened…, I am upset…, my condolences… I feel happy… hurray…)
teaching_learning_activities: Pictures : Display pictures/ emojis representing different feelings for students to describe orally what they represent
Case study/Scenario : Provide the students with a case study/ scenario and ask them to express their feelings orally using appropriate phrases.
Dialogue: Guide the students through acting out a dialogue with appropriate phrases for expressing feelings and emotions.
entries.assessment: Feelings and emotions depicted/portrayed in various social contexts are expressed using appropriate phrases
resources: relevant texts (e.g., books for English grammar)
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 3.1 Use appropriate grammar and vocabulary for oral communication in a variety of contexts
entries.learning_activity: (c) Negotiate solutions to various problems using appropriate formalaic language (e.g., Would you mind…, don’t you think…, is it possible…, if I were you…)
teaching_learning_activities: Roleplay: Guide the students through roleplaying negotiations to various problems using appropriate formulaic language
Dialogue: Guide the students through reading aloud and acting out dialogues by negotiating solutions to various problems using appropriate formulaic language
entries.assessment: Solutions to various problems are negotiated using appropriate formulaic language
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 3.2 Create a variety of texts for different communicative purposes using the appropriate tone and register
entries.learning_activity: (a) Write application letters for various purposes ( e.g., job application, school admission)
teaching_learning_activities: Information search : Guide the students through using various sources to search for information on the types and parts of application letters and presenting their work to the class
Pair work: Guide the students in pairs through writing application letters for various purposes
entries.assessment: Application letters for various purposes are written properly
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 3.2 Create a variety of texts for different communicative purposes using the appropriate tone and register
entries.learning_activity: (b) Write speeches on a variety of topics, including cross-cutting issues
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming : Guide the students through brainstorming the structure of speeches
Demonstration: Demonstrate how to begin writing a speech by asking the students to give ideas and help you plan the speech
Collaborative practice: Guide the students in pairs or groups through writing speeches on a variety of topics, including cross-cutting issues
entries.assessment: Speeches on a variety of topics, including cross-cutting issues, are written properly
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 3.2 Create a variety of texts for different communicative purposes using the appropriate tone and register
entries.learning_activity: (c) Write Curriculum Vitae (CV) for different purposes
teaching_learning_activities: Jigsaw: Guide the students through explaining the meaning, components and the importance of curriculum vitae using jigsaw
Independent practice: Guide individual students through writing their CVs and sharing them in pairs for comments and improvement
Publishing/storing : Guide the students in groups through storing or publishing their CVs on different platforms
entries.assessment: Curriculum Vitae (CV) for different purposes are written properly
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 3.3 Conduct a socio-cultural analysis of functional texts
entries.learning_activity: (a) Analyse different functional texts in terms of purpose, content, organisation, format and design
teaching_learning_activities: Jigsaw : Guide the students through the jigsaw to analyse different functional texts read (in terms of purpose, content, organisation, format and design)
entries.assessment: Different functional texts are analysed correctly in terms of purpose, content, organisation, format and design
resources: Various kinds of functional texts (e.g., recipes, product manuals, transport schedules, forms, brochures, menus)
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 3.3 Conduct a socio-cultural analysis of functional texts
entries.learning_activity: (c) Comment on the usefulness of different functional texts
teaching_learning_activities: Group work: Guide the students in groups through commenting on the usefulness of the functional texts read and presenting their work to the class
entries.assessment: The usefulness of different functional texts is commented on appropriately
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 3.3 Conduct a socio-cultural analysis of functional texts
entries.learning_activity: (d) Compose functional texts to serve various purposes
teaching_learning_activities: Modelling: Engage the students by showing them how to compose various functional texts
Composing: Guide the students through composing various functional texts
entries.assessment: Functional texts are composed properly to serve various purposes
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 4.0 Provide basic English language services to the community
entries.specific_competence: 4.1 Apply principles of editing and proofreading in a variety of texts
entries.learning_activity: (a) Edit and proofread texts in a school context (e.g., school rules and regulations, essays/ compositions)
teaching_learning_activities: Pair work: Guide the students in pairs through editing and proofreading short texts found in the school context and presenting their work to the class
entries.assessment: Texts found in the school context are edited and proofread appropriately
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 4.0 Provide basic English language services to the community
entries.specific_competence: 4.1 Apply principles of editing and proofreading in a variety of texts
entries.learning_activity: (b) Edit and proofread short texts in contexts outside the school (e.g. posters, flyers, user manuals)
teaching_learning_activities: Group work: Guide the students in groups through editing and proofreading short texts found outside the school context and presenting their work to the class
entries.assessment: Texts found outside the school context are edited and proofread appropriately
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 4.0 Provide basic English language services to the community
entries.specific_competence: 4.2 Apply principles of interpretation to provide simple authentic interpretations
entries.learning_activity: (a) Interpret messages in informal contexts (e.g., family conversations and peer conversations) (from English to Kiswahili and vice versa)
teaching_learning_activities: Simultaneous and consecutive interpretation: Guide the students in pairs through simultaneous and consecutive interpretation to interpret messages in informal contexts
entries.assessment: Messages in informal contexts are interpreted correctly (from English to Kiswahili and vice versa)
resources: audio and audio-visual materials (audio/video clips, recorded stories)
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 4.0 Provide basic English language services to the community
entries.specific_competence: 4.2 Apply principles of interpretation to provide simple authentic interpretations
entries.learning_activity: (b) Interpret messages in small formal settings (e.g., classroom and subject group discussions) (from English to Kiswahili and vice versa)
teaching_learning_activities: Roleplay: Guide the students in pairs through roleplaying the role of the speaker and interpreter in interpreting messages in small formal settings
entries.assessment: Messages in small formal settings are interpreted correctly (from English to Kiswahili and vice versa)
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 4.0 Provide basic English language services to the community
entries.specific_competence: 4.2 Apply principles of interpretation to provide simple authentic interpretations
entries.learning_activity: (c) Interpret messages in large formal settings (e.g., church, mosque and meetings) (from English to Kiswahili and vice versa).
teaching_learning_activities: Simultaneous and consecutive interpretation: Guide the students in pairs through interpreting messages in informal contexts using simultaneous and consecutive interpretation
entries.assessment: Messages in large formal settings are interpreted correctly (from English to Kiswahili and vice versa)
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 4.0 Provide basic English language services to the community
entries.specific_competence: 4.3 Apply principles of translation to produce simple authentic translation
entries.learning_activity: (a) Translate texts in contexts outside the school (from English to Kiswahili and vice versa)
teaching_learning_activities: Collaborative and independent practice: Guide the students in groups and individually through translating texts found outside the school context using communicative translation
entries.assessment: Texts found outside the school context are translated correctly (from English to Kiswahili and vice versa)
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 4.0 Provide basic English language services to the community
entries.specific_competence: 4.3 Apply principles of translation to produce simple authentic translation
entries.learning_activity: (b) Translate a variety of short texts (e.g., advertisements and functional texts) (from English to Kiswahili and vice versa)
teaching_learning_activities: Collaborative and independent practice: Guide the students in groups and individually through translating a variety of short texts using communicative translation
entries.assessment: A variety of short texts are translated correctly (from English to Kiswahili and vice versa)
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 4.0 Provide basic English language services to the community
entries.specific_competence: 4.3 Apply principles of translation to produce simple authentic translation
entries.learning_activity: (c) Translate a variety of long texts (from English to Kiswahili and vice versa)
teaching_learning_activities: Collaborative and independent practice: Guide the students in pairs and individually through translating a variety of long texts using communicative translation
entries.assessment: A variety of long texts are translated correctly (from English to Kiswahili and vice versa)
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.main_competence: 4.0 Provide basic English language services to the community
entries.specific_competence: 4.4 Manage short translation and editing projects using Computer-Assisted Tools
entries.learning_activity: (a) Desribe the use of Computer-Assisted Tools in translating and editing
teaching_learning_activities: Group work: Guide the students in groups through describing the use of Computer Assisted Tools in translating and editing
entries.assessment: The use of Computer-Assisted Tools in translating and editing is described correctly
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 5.1 Appreciate the aesthetics and value of literature
entries.learning_activity: (a) Assess the language used in a selected novella
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming: Guide the students through brainstorming techniques for analysing language in texts
Close reading: Guide the students through assessing the language used in the novella read and presenting their work to the class
entries.assessment: The language used in the selected novella is assessed appropriately
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 5.1 Appreciate the aesthetics and value of literature
entries.learning_activity: (b) Compare own values and perspectives with those implied in a selected novella
teaching_learning_activities: TIPS: Guide the students through identifying their own values and perspectives using TIPS
Pair work: Guide the students in pairs through comparing own values and perspectives with those implied in the selected novella
entries.assessment: Own values and perspectives are compared appropriately with those implied in the selected novella
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 5.2 Evaluate the context in which literary texts are written, read and understood
entries.learning_activity: (a) Examine the socio-political and cultural issues portrayed in a selected novella
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming: Guide the students in groups through brainstorming ideas about socio-political and cultural issues and presenting their work to the class
Group work: Guide the students in groups through examining the socio-political and cultural issues portrayed in the selected novella and presenting their work to the class
entries.assessment: The socio-political and cultural issues portrayed in the selected novella are examined properly
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 5.2 Evaluate the context in which literary texts are written, read and understood
entries.learning_activity: (a) Examine the socio-political and cultural issues portrayed in a selected novella
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming: Guide the students in groups through brainstorming ideas about socio-political and cultural issues and presenting their work to the class
Group work: Guide the students in groups through examining the socio-political and cultural issues portrayed in the selected novella and presenting their work to the class
entries.assessment: The socio-political and cultural issues portrayed in the selected novella are examined properly
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 5.2 Evaluate the context in which literary texts are written, read and understood
entries.learning_activity: (b) Relate the context of the story in a selected novella to real life experiences
teaching_learning_activities: Discussion: Guide the students through discussing the context of a story in the selected novella, relating it to real-life experiences and presenting their work to the class
entries.assessment: The context of the story in the selected novella is related appropriately to real-life experiences
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 5.3 Analyse genres of literature and appreciate their conventions
entries.learning_activity: (a) Analyse techniques of critiquing literary texts based on their conventions
teaching_learning_activities: Gallery walk: Guide the students through analysing and presenting techniques for critiquing literary texts using gallery walk
entries.assessment: Techniques for critiquing literary texts based on their conventions are analysed appropriately
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 5.4 Create simple literary works
entries.learning_activity: (a) Apply literary skills to compose short stories on various topics
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming: Guide the students through brainstorming ideas on the skills required in composing short stories and presenting the work to the class
Collaborative and independent practice: Guide the students in groups and individually through composing short stories on various topics
Follow-up: Check the correctness of the stories(e.g., a series of events, coherence and language used)
entries.assessment: Literary skills are applied appropriately to compose short stories on various topics
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 5.4 Create simple literary works
entries.learning_activity: (a) Apply literary skills to compose short stories on various topics
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming: Guide the students through brainstorming ideas on the skills required in composing short stories and presenting the work to the class
Collaborative and independent practice: Guide the students in groups and individually through composing short stories on various topics
Follow-up: Check the correctness of the stories(e.g., a series of events, coherence and language used)
entries.assessment: Literary skills are applied appropriately to compose short stories on various topics
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 5.4 Create simple literary works
entries.learning_activity: (b) Publish the short stories on various platforms (e.g., note boards and online platforms)
teaching_learning_activities: Project work : Guide the students through publishing the short stories they have created on various platforms (e.g., note boards and the Internet)
entries.assessment: Short stories are published appropriately on various platforms
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)

entries.specific_competence: 5.4 Create simple literary works
entries.learning_activity: (b) Publish the short stories on various platforms (e.g., note boards and online platforms)
teaching_learning_activities: Project work : Guide the students through publishing the short stories they have created on various platforms (e.g., note boards and the Internet)
entries.assessment: Short stories are published appropriately on various platforms
entries.references: English Language Syllabus for Ordinary Secondary Education, 2023 (Tanzania Institute of Education)
